subject: PhD Analysis
instructions:
Be sure to write all steps in a neat and logical fashion to receive credit. Give reasons for your steps.

Definition: A σ-algebra \(\mathcal F\) is separable if \(\mathcal F=\sigma\{A_j:j\geq 1\}\) for some sequence of sets \(A_j\).

problems.text: Let \(\{\mathcal F_k\}\) be a sequence of separable σ-algebras of subsets of a set \(X\). Let \(\mathcal F\) be the smallest σ-algebra of subsets of \(X\) which contains \(\mathcal F_k\) for each \(k\). Prove that \(\mathcal F\) is separable. [Hint: Use the monotone class theorem.]

Let \((X,\mathcal F,\mu)\) be a finite measure space and let \((Y,\Sigma)\) be a measurable space. Let \(f:X	o Y\) be \(\mathcal F,\Sigma\)-measurable. Define \(
u(B)=\mu(f^{-1}(B))\) for \(B\in\Sigma\). Show \(
u\) is a measure on \(\Sigma\), and if \(g:Y	o[0,\infty]\) is \(\Sigma\)-measurable, then \(\int_Y g\,d
u=\int_X g\circ f\,d\mu\).
Let \(m\) be Lebesgue measure on \(\mathbb R\). Let \(f_m=-\mathbf 1_{A_m}\), where \(A_m=(m,\infty)\). Show \((f_m)\) is increasing, but the monotone convergence theorem is not true. Why is this?

Let \((X,\mathcal S,\mu)\) and \((Y,\mathcal J,
u)\) be two finite measure spaces. Construct the product measure \(\mu	imes
u\) on \(\mathcal S\otimes\mathcal J\). Be sure to prove \(\mu	imes
u\) is countably additive.
Let \((X,\Sigma,\mu)\) be a finite measure space. Prove that the dual of \(L^1(X,\Sigma,\mu)\) is isometrically isomorphic to \(L^\infty(X,\Sigma,\mu)\).
Let \((X,\mathcal F,\mu)\) be a finite measure space. Suppose that \(\mathcal F\) is separable. Show \(L^1(X,\mathcal F,\mu)\) is separable.

Let \((X,\mathcal F,\mu)\) be a measure space. Let \(\{f_m\}\) be a sequence of measurable functions such that \(\int_X|f_m|\,d\mu<\frac{1}{2^m}\). Does it follow that \(\sum_{m=1}^\infty f_m\) converges a.e. \(\mu\) on \(X\)?
Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,P)\) be a probability space. Let \(X:\Omega\to\mathbb R\) be an integrable function. Let \(F(x)=P(X<x)\), for \(x\in\mathbb R\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show \(F:\mathbb R\to[0,1]\) is monotone, left continuous, \(\lim_{x\to-\infty}F(x)=0\), and \(\lim_{x\to\infty}F(x)=1\).

problems.text: Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,P)\) be a probability space. Let \((X_m)\) be a sequence of independent random variables on \(\Omega\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show \((\overline{\lim}X_m>3)\) is a tail event relative to \((X_m)\).

problems.text: Let \(\ell^\infty\) be the Banach space consisting of all real sequences \((a_i)\) such that \(\sup_i|a_i|<\infty\), with \(\|(a_i)\|:=\sup_i|a_i|\). Let \(c\) be the subspace of \(\ell^\infty\) consisting of \((a_i)\) such that \(\lim a_i\) exists. Prove that there exists a continuous linear functional \(F:\ell^\infty\to\mathbb R\) such that \(F((a_i))=\lim a_i\) for each \((a_i)\in c\). [Hint: Use the Hahn–Banach theorem.]
